subject: docs(styles): trim styles.css from 521 → 164 lines
body:
Audit finding #13 — styles.css carried ~360 lines of "tombstone"
comments documenting CSS classes that were deleted during the
2026-04-13/14 vanilla-DaisyUI sweep. Each deleted class had a
multi-line block describing what it used to do and what it migrated
to, layered on top of months of similar archaeology from earlier
refactors.

The tombstones served their purpose (helped reviewers verify nothing
was missed during the sweep) but they're now dead context — every
deleted class is gone, every consumer is on DaisyUI utilities, and
the migration history lives in HISTORY.md and git log. Keeping them
inflates the file 3.4x with no consumer benefit.

Kept (everything in the file is still load-bearing):
  - @import "tailwindcss"
  - @source not data dirs (phantom utility prevention)
  - DaisyUI @plugin block with BEGIN/END GENERATED markers
    (unchanged — generator regenerates this on every build)
  - @custom-variant dark
  - @layer base color-scheme
  - :root design tokens (spacing, z-index scale, radius, fonts)
    — z-index comment retained at full length per #18
  - body safe-area padding + bg/color
  - Element-selector exceptions block:
      * { font-family: var(--font-sans) } reset
      h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-mono) } headings
      body::before { decorative grid background }
    Each rule keeps a one-paragraph rationale (down from
    multi-paragraph blocks) so the "why this is a documented
    exception" context survives.
  - @media print { element selectors only }
  - @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { universal selector }

Removed:
  - ~50 deleted-class tombstone comments (.tag, .panel, .timeline-dot,
    .filter-sidebar, .drop-zone, .toast, .modal, .skeleton,
    .heatmap-*, .collapsible-*, .stat-*, .tab-btn-*,
    .scrollbar-hide, .filter-badge, .install-modal-*, .root-error-*,
    .error-boundary-card, .project-*, .quick-guide-*, .show-more-btn,
    .hamburger-*, ...) — every one of these classes is gone from the
    JSX. The migration target is documented in the consumer file's
    requirement/approach comment block instead.
  - Dead "Deleted blocks that used to live here" archaeology paragraphs
    pointing at Tailwind gray overrides, semantic card backgrounds,
    text color overrides, and `.dashboard-enter` animations — none of
    these have left a trace in JSX or CSS.
  - Verbose 4-paragraph element-selector exceptions intro shrunk to
    one paragraph per rule (still documents alternative rejected).

Build: 147.09 kB → 147.17 kB (+0.08 kB, comment-stripped CSS but
slightly different whitespace after rewrite). Functionally identical.
60/60 tests pass — including the styles.css allowlist test, which
confirms zero custom CSS classes remain.

Tags: documentation, cleanup, css
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-cleanup
Semver: patch
